etymology.description: 點(點) (diǎn, "dot; point; prepare delicately") + 茶(茶) (chá, "tea") refers to the traditional method of preparing tea by whisking it into a frothy mixture.
etymology.interpretation: 點(點) (diǎn, "dot; point; prepare delicately") can convey the sense of a delicate and refined process, while 茶(茶) (chá, "tea") specifies the substance being prepared. This combination reflects a time-honored technique of whisking powdered tea with water to create a frothy, aromatic drink, a practice prevalent during the Song Dynasty, illustrating the importance of tea in Chinese culture and its artistic presentation.

examples.chinese: 点茶是一种古老而优雅的茶艺(點茶是一種古老而優雅的茶藝)。
examples.pinyin: Diǎn chá shì yī zhǒng gǔlǎo ér yōuyǎ de cháyì.
examples.english: Tea whisking is an ancient and elegant tea art.

examples.chinese: 在茶室里，我们学习如何点茶(在茶室裏，我們學習如何點茶)。
examples.pinyin: Zài cháshì lǐ, wǒmen xuéxí rúhé diǎn chá.
examples.english: In the tea room, we learn how to whisk tea.

examples.chinese: 点茶的过程要求细心和耐心(點茶的過程要求細心和耐心)。
examples.pinyin: Diǎn chá de guòchéng yāoqiú xìxīn hé nàixīn.
examples.english: The process of tea whisking requires attentiveness and patience.
